page: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram is a fractional GPU instance within the Cloud GPU family. It features 4 shared vCPUs based on the Intel x86_64 Broadwell architecture running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 20.0 GB of system memory. The hardware configuration includes a fractional slice of 0.1667 of an NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU, providing 8 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is supported by a 360 GB NVMe SSD.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in specific workloads, particularly LLM inference. For a small 135M model, the instance achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds of 9881.81 tokens per second and text generation speeds of 465.27 tokens per second. With a medium 7B model, it maintains top-tier performance, processing 3161.22 tokens per second for prompts and generating 107.91 tokens per second. Conversely, general CPU and memory tasks show average to weak performance. Passmark CPU encryption tests and database operations are rated as weak, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor.

This instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads by utilizing a shared CPU allocation and a fractional GPU slice. It avoids the higher costs associated with provisioning a full, dedicated NVIDIA A40 GPU. The resource profile is highly suitable for lightweight machine learning inference, small-scale LLM execution, and development environments requiring CUDA acceleration, though it is less optimal for heavy multi-threaded CPU processing or high-throughput database operations.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram is a fractional GPU instance designed for entry-level accelerated workloads. It is equipped with 4 shared Intel x86_64 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 20.0 GB of system memory, and a 360 GB NVMe SSD. The instance features a fractional 0.1667 slice of an NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU, delivering 8 GB of VRAM. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in LLM inference, achieving 3161.22 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 7B model. However, general compute and database operations show average to weak performance, with weak results in Passmark database tests. This configuration provides a cost-effective option for developers needing GPU acceleration without the expense of a full dedicated card. It is best suited for small-scale machine learning inference, CUDA development, and lightweight AI workloads.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram is a fractional GPU server featuring 4 Intel vCPUs, 20 GB RAM, a 360 GB NVMe SSD, and a 0.1667 slice of an NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 8 GB VRAM for entry-level AI workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram is a fractional GPU instance with 4 Intel vCPUs, 20 GB RAM, and 8 GB of NVIDIA Ampere A40 VRAM for lightweight AI inference.
tagline: A fractional GPU instance combining Intel processors and NVIDIA Ampere acceleration for cost-effective machine learning inference and CUDA development.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU providing 8 GB of dedicated VRAM
4 shared Intel x86_64 vCPUs paired with 20.0 GB of system memory
360 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for fast data access
Top-tier LLM inference performance for small and medium models
Cost-effective resource profile for entry-level GPU workloads
Best suited for lightweight machine learning, AI inference, and CUDA development
